body:
This PRD was selected from the durable 2026-07-17 Unbidden Oath author-playtest report after closed PRD #91 consumed the report's first PRD candidate and explicitly deferred F006. On 2026-07-18, the user ratified this single intended PRD, the archive-derived coherence rule, the manual recovery behavior, the existing API/core/component testing seams, and publication with `bug` plus `ready-for-agent`. The durable originating evidence is `reports/playtest-the-unbidden-oath-2026-07-17T104952Z.md`; the local prep artifact was summarized, not cited.

## Problem Statement

After an author accepts the first prose segment, the saved Generation Brief can continue to carry `first_segment` as its generation context. The current system derives `continuation_after_accepted_segment` from accepted-segment count only when the draft omits a context; an explicit saved value is preserved even when it contradicts the archive.

That mismatch lets readiness, Prompt Preview, and Generate evaluate the next request under first-segment rules after accepted prose exists. Continuation-only handoff requirements can therefore be bypassed until the author notices the stale selector and changes it manually. The inverse mismatch is also possible after the final accepted segment is deleted: a saved continuation context can outlive the archive fact that the next request is again a first segment.

The author currently sees a saved value and an accepted-segment count, but no explicit coherence diagnosis or canonical recovery action. This weakens the deterministic lifecycle promised by `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md`, conflicts with the stale-context case in `docs/stress-suite.md`, and makes a validation-only control behave like an unchecked author preference instead of a truthful description of project state.

## Solution

Make accepted-segment archive state and Generation Brief context a deterministic coherence contract.

- Zero accepted segments requires `first_segment`.
- One or more accepted segments requires `continuation_after_accepted_segment`.
- A missing saved context continues to receive the archive-derived default.
- A contradictory saved context remains structurally saveable and is never silently rewritten, but it produces a readiness blocker before Prompt Preview, compilation, candidate intake, Generate, or provider transport.
- Readiness evaluates the archive-derived required context even while the saved value is contradictory, so continuation or first-segment requirements cannot be bypassed by stale draft state.
- The author sees both the saved value and the archive-derived required value in author language, together with a direct action to the existing Generation Brief context selector.
- The author clears the mismatch by choosing the required value and explicitly saving the draft.

The same rule applies symmetrically when the first segment is accepted and when deletion removes the final accepted segment. Additional acceptances while at least one segment remains do not create a new transition. The system uses accepted-segment count only; it never reads accepted prose text, infers handoff content, updates story records, or mutates the Generation Brief automatically.

Because this changes accepted-segment lifecycle behavior, readiness normalization, validation, and browser-visible recovery, implementation begins with a new active spec registered in the active-doc registry. The spec pins the state matrix, diagnostic contract, existing-route metadata, recovery behavior, synchronized authority updates, and regression plan before production behavior changes.

## User Stories

1. As an author, I want the app to recognize that accepting my first segment changes the next generation into a continuation, so that I cannot unknowingly generate under first-segment rules.
2. As an author, I want the app to recognize that deleting the final accepted segment changes the next generation back into a first segment, so that continuation-only rules do not survive without accepted prose.
3. As an author, I want a missing generation context to resolve from accepted-segment count, so that ordinary incomplete drafts receive a deterministic non-story default.
4. As an author, I want a contradictory saved context to remain in my draft until I explicitly change it, so that lifecycle checks never rewrite project data behind my back.
5. As an author, I want a contradictory saved context to block Prompt Preview and Generate, so that the app never validates or compiles against the wrong lifecycle rules.
6. As an author, I want readiness to use the archive-derived required context while reporting the mismatch, so that stale first-segment state cannot suppress continuation handoff requirements.
7. As an author, I want to see the saved context and the archive-derived required context together, so that I understand exactly why the draft is blocked.
8. As an author, I want the mismatch explanation in author language, so that I do not need to interpret raw enum values or diagnostic codes.
9. As an author, I want the readiness action to focus the existing generation-context control, so that I can repair the canonical field without searching the page.
10. As an author, I want to choose the required context and save it myself, so that the lifecycle fix remains an explicit author action.
11. As an author, I want structurally saveable incomplete drafts to remain saveable during a mismatch, so that validation never destroys or rejects ordinary work in progress.
12. As an author, I want the mismatch to clear deterministically after save and reload, so that I can trust the displayed readiness state.
13. As an author, I want continuation handoff blockers to remain visible after the context mismatch is fixed, so that accepted prose never substitutes for user-authored launch context.
14. As an author, I want accepted prose text excluded from every future prose prompt, so that lifecycle coherence does not weaken the prose-as-output boundary.
15. As an author, I want checking or repairing context coherence to remain local, so that no OpenRouter request occurs before I explicitly choose Generate.
16. As an author, I want Generate to make no provider call while context is incoherent, so that a stale draft cannot cross the user-initiated network boundary.
17. As an author, I want accepting a second or later segment to preserve the already-correct continuation context, so that stable archive state does not create needless blockers.
18. As an author, I want deleting a non-final segment to preserve continuation readiness, so that only the zero-versus-nonzero archive boundary changes the required context.
19. As an author opening an existing project, I want stale saved contexts to load without migration or data loss, so that I can repair them through the normal Generation Brief workflow.
20. As an author, I want the same mismatch diagnosis on Generation Brief, Prompt Preview, and Generate, so that readiness does not disagree across pages.
21. As a keyboard or assistive-technology user, I want the mismatch, required value, and repair action to have accessible names and focus behavior, so that recovery is operable without pointer-only discovery.
22. As a maintainer, I want one deterministic zero-versus-nonzero state matrix, so that acceptance, deletion, reload, validation, compilation, and generation cannot drift into different lifecycle rules.
23. As a maintainer, I want one stable diagnostic code and inventory entry for an archive-to-draft mismatch, so that API, readiness, documentation, and regression coverage stay synchronized.
24. As a maintainer, I want acceptance and deletion to leave the saved Generation Brief untouched, so that accepted-segment storage and author-owned generation fields remain separate transaction boundaries.
25. As a maintainer, I want existing API route families and component seams reused, so that this correction adds no duplicate lifecycle authority or test-only interface.
26. As a maintainer, I want the broad behavior pinned in an active spec before implementation, so that schema, validation, UI, and documentation changes share one approved contract.

## Implementation Decisions

1. **Accepted-segment count is the lifecycle fact.** The required generation context is derived only from whether the accepted-segment archive count is zero or nonzero. Segment text, provenance, sequence contents, candidates, reminders, and assistance output do not participate in the derivation. This follows the deterministic non-story-default rule in `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md`.
2. **Draft persistence and readiness remain separate.** A structurally valid draft may store either context value regardless of the current archive count. Saving remains available; incoherence gates only readiness-dependent operations. This preserves the project's established draftability contract.
3. **Mismatch fails closed.** Add one blocker diagnostic with the stable code `generation-context-accepted-segment-mismatch`. Its affected target is the Generation Brief context field; primary copy names the saved state, required state, accepted-segment count, and fastest repair in author language. The technical code remains detail-level metadata and is added to `docs/validation-rule-inventory.md`.
4. **Required context governs evaluation.** When a saved value contradicts archive state, the validation snapshot uses the archive-derived required context for all other deterministic checks while also emitting the mismatch blocker. This prevents stale state from suppressing continuation handoff requirements or incorrectly retaining them after final-segment deletion.
5. **No automatic draft mutation.** Accepting or deleting a segment changes only the accepted-segment boundary already owned by those operations. Validation, compilation, readiness reads, and Generation Brief reads do not rewrite the saved draft. This manual-fix rule was recommended by the report determination and explicitly ratified at the publication checkpoint.
6. **Existing routes expose truthful coherence metadata.** The existing Generation Brief response must expose the saved value when present, the archive-derived required value, accepted-segment count, and whether they are coherent. The required spec chooses one canonical response shape and removes any misleading single-value/default interpretation; it must not retain parallel compatibility fields as duplicate authority.
7. **The canonical fix is the existing editor.** Readiness actions route to and focus the current Generation Brief context selector. The author chooses the required value and invokes the ordinary Save Generation Brief action. No one-click auto-fix, background transition, acceptance-side write, or assistance prefill is introduced.
8. **Zero/nonzero transitions are symmetric.** First acceptance can create a `first_segment` mismatch; final-segment deletion can create a `continuation_after_accepted_segment` mismatch. Accepting another segment or deleting a non-final segment leaves the required continuation state unchanged.
9. **All readiness-dependent entry points share the blocker.** Prompt Preview, deterministic compilation, user-supplied candidate intake, Generate, and provider sending consume the same readiness result. A mismatch permits no prompt bytes or provider transport and requires an explicit save followed by fresh compilation.
10. **Accepted prose remains output, not context.** The coherence check reads only the count. It never copies, parses, summarizes, embeds, or derives a field from accepted prose, and it does not expand Segment Reconciliation's narrow assistance exception.
11. **No storage migration is required.** Existing enum values and generation-session persistence remain valid. Older projects load normally; contradictory state becomes actionable through readiness. No accepted-segment, export, provenance, or project-schema column changes are introduced.
12. **Spec before behavior.** A new active spec is registered before production edits. It defines the complete state matrix, diagnostic copy contract, route metadata, effective-readiness rule, no-mutation assertions, browser-visible recovery, synchronized docs, and completion/archive procedure.
13. **Authorities change together.** The Compiler Contract, Definitive Story Record Schema, `docs/validation-rule-inventory.md`, `docs/stress-suite.md`, `docs/stress-coverage-matrix.md`, `docs/demo-blocker-recipes.md`, and `docs/user-guide.md` are synchronized in the behavior revision. `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md` is expected to require no amendment because the PRD enforces its current normalization, draftability, accepted-prose, and fail-closed rules.
14. **Browser behavior is additive and focused.** Navigation, accepted-segment controls, and Generate workflow remain in place. The new visible behavior is the coherent/mismatched state, author-language explanation, direct field focus, manual save, and shared readiness gating.
15. **No open product mechanism remains.** Exact internal helper boundaries and the final canonical response property names may be refined by the required spec, but they must satisfy the ratified state matrix, one-authority rule, diagnostic, manual recovery, no-mutation boundary, and existing testing seams. This is bounded implementation discretion, not an open scope decision.

### Browser-visible guidance checklist mapping

| Canonical item | PRD home |
| --- | --- |
| `entry point and availability` | Solution, User Stories 1-3 and 20, and Implementation Decisions 7-9 preserve Generation Brief, Prompt Preview, and Generate as the existing entry points and define when incoherence blocks readiness-dependent actions. |
| `user-visible states, actions, and outcomes` | Solution, User Stories 7-12, and Implementation Decisions 6, 7, and 14 define saved, required, coherent, mismatched, focused repair, explicit save, reload, and cleared outcomes. |
| `validation, warning, error, and recovery behavior` | User Stories 5-13 and Implementation Decisions 3, 4, 7, and 9 define the blocker, author-language copy, continuation checks, manual correction, fresh save, and recompile recovery. |
| `prompt preview contents and freshness` | Solution and Implementation Decisions 4 and 9 require zero prompt bytes while incoherent and a fresh compile only after the saved value matches archive-derived state. |
| `user-initiated external LLM boundary` | User Stories 15 and 16 plus Implementation Decision 9 require local coherence checks, no provider call while blocked, and unchanged explicit Generate initiation after recovery. |
| `canon and prose boundary visibility` | User Stories 13 and 14 plus Implementation Decisions 1 and 10 state that only accepted-segment count is read and accepted prose remains output excluded from prose prompts and automatic field derivation. |
| `persistence, migration, export, and provenance` | User Stories 4, 11, 19, and 24 plus Implementation Decisions 2, 5, and 11 preserve the saved draft until explicit author save and introduce no migration, export, accepted-segment, or provenance change. |
| `browser and accessibility regression scenario` | Testing Decisions 4 and 5 reuse existing Generation Brief and cross-page readiness component seams for author-language status, keyboard focus, accessible names, manual save, and shared gating; no new browser framework or full browser journey is required. |

## Testing Decisions

Tests assert externally observable lifecycle, readiness, persistence, API, and browser behavior rather than private helper structure. Seam confirmation: on 2026-07-18, the user ratified the existing server API lifecycle seam, core readiness/snapshot seam, Generation Brief component seam, and cross-page readiness component seam. No new public test interface, browser framework, full browser journey, or external-model call is introduced.

1. **Server API lifecycle seam.** Extend the existing server generation-brief draftability capstone to start with zero accepted segments and a saved first-segment draft, accept the first segment, and observe the unchanged saved value, required continuation value, mismatch blocker, continuation-specific readiness checks, blocked compile, blocked Generate, and zero provider calls. After an explicit context save and reload, prove the mismatch clears and valid continuation input compiles without accepted prose.
2. **Symmetric deletion seam.** Through the same existing API family, delete the final accepted segment from a saved continuation draft and observe required first-segment state, unchanged persistence, the mismatch blocker, blocked readiness-dependent operations, manual correction, reload, and recovery. Also prove additional acceptance and non-final deletion do not create false transitions.
3. **Core state-matrix seam.** Extend the existing generation-brief readiness normalization and server snapshot tests across missing, matching, and mismatched saved context with zero, one, and multiple accepted segments. Assert one deterministic required context, one mismatch diagnostic when applicable, no false `focus-tag-count-invalid`, and archive-derived evaluation of continuation or first-segment requirements.
4. **Generation Brief component seam.** Reuse existing component coverage to show saved and required values, accepted-segment count, coherent and mismatched states, author-language explanation, accessible context control, direct focus, explicit save, save result, and reload result. Draft Save remains available while Preview and Generate remain blocked.
5. **Cross-page readiness seam.** Reuse the existing Generation Brief, Prompt Preview, and Generate readiness component matrix to prove the same blocker copy, target, action, counts, and technical details appear consistently; keyboard activation reaches the context field; no stale page claims readiness.
6. **Persistence and no-mutation seam.** At the server repository boundary, compare generation-session rows before and after acceptance, deletion, readiness, compile, and blocked Generate. Only the accepted-segment operation changes its own rows; only the explicit Generation Brief save changes the stored context.
7. **Accepted-prose firewall.** Use a unique accepted-prose sentinel and prove it appears in neither validation output, Generation Brief coherence metadata, compiled prose prompt, logs, nor provider request. Only accepted-segment count influences the coherence rule.
8. **Diagnostic and documentation conformance.** Update the diagnostic inventory drift check, Case 28 and Case 29 stress expectations, their coverage-matrix rows, a demo blocker recipe, and user-facing guidance in the same behavior revision. The required spec and authority pack are reviewed for the complete zero/nonzero matrix and no-mutation rule.
9. **Canonical gates.** Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm test`, and `npm run build`.

## Principles

- `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md`: the design passes the Section 29 alignment checklist. It makes generation context depend on deterministic accepted-segment state, preserves structurally saveable drafts, fails closed before prompt/provider operations, keeps accepted prose out of future prompts, and requires explicit author action for the only project-data change.
- Compiler Contract: the prose source profile continues to consume a normalized ready input rather than UI-only defaults. The validation matrix gains the archive-to-draft coherence blocker without making validation tags prompt-facing.
- Definitive Story Record Schema: draft and ready shapes remain distinct; existing generation-context enum values remain valid; the accepted-segment count supplies the required non-story lifecycle fact; no storage migration or accepted-prose source is added.
- `docs/validation-rule-inventory.md`: the new blocker code, severity, description, constitutional clause, and stress coverage land in the same behavior revision.
- `docs/stress-suite.md` and `docs/stress-coverage-matrix.md`: stale-context prevention and continuation defaults become an explicit symmetric acceptance/deletion matrix rather than an omitted-context-only default.
- `docs/demo-blocker-recipes.md`: a normal-edit recipe demonstrates a saveable mismatch, blocked Preview/Generate, author-language recovery, and deterministic clearing after explicit save.
- `docs/user-guide.md`: user-facing guidance explains required context, mismatch blocking, saved-versus-required display, manual repair, accepted-prose exclusion, and zero provider calls before Generate.
- No domain glossary or ADR directory exists in this checkout. No deliberate exception to an active principle or authority is approved. If implementation proves that `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md` requires amendment, work stops for the explicit amendment procedure rather than weakening this PRD silently.

## Out of Scope

- Automatically changing the saved generation context during acceptance, deletion, project open, readiness, compilation, reminder display, or Segment Reconciliation
- Inferring, copying, summarizing, or prefilling immediate handoff or current state from accepted prose
- Automatically updating story records, active-working-set membership, reminders, or canonical editors
- Changing accepted-segment text, source provenance, sequence numbering, archive ordering, export, or deletion confirmation
- Changing Segment Reconciliation sources, proposals, application boundaries, or acknowledgement behavior
- Changing the two existing generation-context enum values or making context a story-record field
- Adding a project schema migration, compatibility alias, duplicate response authority, or persisted derived context
- Changing provider selection, OpenRouter transport, retry behavior, prompt persistence, candidate persistence, or model-quality policy
- Bundling Voice Pressure Cast Picker, Desktop Navigation Reachability, Cast Creation Prerequisite Guidance, Prompt Search Match Navigation, Story Configuration Save-State Consistency, Cast Record Scanability, note-identity verification, reconciliation no-op verification, or Ideate slot-completeness work
- Revisiting covered, rejected, preference-only, or strength-preservation findings from the source report

## Further Notes

- The Step 2 testing-seam and publication-package checkpoint was answered on 2026-07-18. The user ratified one PRD, the exact zero/nonzero coherence rule, saveable-but-blocking mismatch behavior, manual selector-and-save recovery, the existing server/core/component seams, no new browser journey or model call, and publication with `bug` plus `ready-for-agent`.
- Closed PRD #91 and children #92/#93 consumed the bounded reconciliation-catalog candidate and explicitly left Accepted-Segment Generation Context Coherence out of scope. This is the only remaining PRD-ready candidate from the report; the report's other live items retain their ticket, coverage, skill-maintenance, preservation, or no-op destinations.
- The durable source report is tracked, clean, visible on `origin/main`, and content-identical there. The local prep artifact is untracked and was used only for synthesis; it is summarized, not cited.
- The source report's validator has one nonblocking methodology disclosure defect concerning a declared counterfactual probe. Its complete F001-F021 inventory remains safely inspectable, and the defect is routed to skill maintenance rather than this product PRD.
- The current local Compiler Contract and Definitive Story Record Schema include closed #91 revisions that are not yet reachable from `origin/main`. Their relevant generation-context doctrine is summarized here rather than citing those local-only revisions as durable source text.
- The exact-title duplicate guard returned no existing issue with this title before staging. Current same-kind issue metadata proves that `bug` and `ready-for-agent` exist. Every applicable browser-visible guidance item has a concrete home above.
- No product-scope, testing-seam, checklist, label, mechanism, or source decision remains provisional, timed out, or open to veto.

labels.name: needs-triage
labels.description: Maintainer needs to evaluate this issue

title: PRD: Accepted-Segment Generation Context Coherence — Fail Closed on Archive/Draft Mismatches
